questions.text: Read the function below. What bug would make this fail for an empty list?
questions.answer: The function divides by len(values), so an empty list causes a division-by-zero error. A guard clause or explicit empty-list policy is needed.
questions.imageAlt: A small diagram showing values flowing into an average function
questions.answerImageAlt: The same diagram can also be configured as a post-grading reveal image

questions.text: The chart shows the order that three async tasks completed. Explain why the printed output might not match the order the tasks were started.
questions.answer: All tasks start before the loop awaits them, so completion order can differ from start order. The loop logs in array order, not completion order.

questions.text: No image on this one. What does this SQL query return, and what edge case should you watch for?
questions.code:
SELECT department, COUNT(*) AS total
FROM employees
WHERE active = true
GROUP BY department
ORDER BY total DESC;
questions.answer: It returns active employee counts grouped by department, ordered largest to smallest. Watch for NULL departments being grouped together.
